{"step_by_step_thinking": "Selenocysteine is a rare amino acid that is incorporated into proteins during ribosomal protein synthesis. Multiple documents confirm that selenocysteine is recognized as one of the 21st amino acids, alongside pyrrolysine. Document [5] explicitly states: 'Selenocysteine occurs at the active site of several enzymes, including thioredoxin reductase and glutathione peroxidase' and'selenocysteine and pyrrolysine are considered the 21st and 22nd amino acids, respectively.' Document [17] further supports this by stating: 'Selenocysteine and pyrrolysine are considered the 21st and 22nd amino acids, respectively. More recently discovered amino acids may be incorporated into protein chains during ribosomal protein synthesis.' Although selenocysteine is not one of the standard 20 amino acids used in protein synthesis, it is a naturally occurring amino acid that is incorporated into proteins in specific contexts. Therefore, based on the scientific literature provided, selenocysteine is classified as an amino acid.", "answer_choice": "A"}
